summary: Frontend-only smoke test after date-fns@^3.6.0 + react-day-picker@^9.4.0 dependency bump. All requested flows verified: (1) landing renders with hero-search-input, (2) admin login via demo-admin-btn+login-submit-btn redirects to /admin with all 7 tabs present (dashboard/drivers/vehicles/routes/users/live/reports) and dashboard stats visible (2 drivers, 3 vehicles, 6 routes, 1 live), (3) driver login via demo-driver-btn redirects to /driver with go-live-btn (shown when offline) plus driver-vehicle-select and driver-route-select populated (UP14AB1234 Bus, Ghaziabad→Noida), (4) /passenger renders .leaflet-container with dark tile layer and 'No live vehicles' empty state, (5) /register renders and all 7 required testids present after minor testid rename fix. ZERO react-day-picker / date-fns runtime errors in browser console. Frontend webpack compiled with just a pre-existing eslint hooks-exhaustive-deps warning in DriverPage.jsx (unrelated to dep bump).

issues: React hydration warning in console: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>' — a Select/Option component is rendering a span inside a native <option>. Non-fatal but pollutes console.
4x 401 responses in console at load — likely /api/auth/me being called without token on public pages. Harmless but noisy.

action_items: OPTIONAL (low priority): fix DriverPage.jsx line 94 useEffect missing 'session' dependency warning.
OPTIONAL (low priority): suppress or gate /api/auth/me call so it doesn't 401 on public pages (LandingPage, Register, Login, Passenger).
OPTIONAL (low priority): fix hydration warning about <span> child of <option> — likely in a shadcn Select component rendering.
User's request to 'commit and push to GitHub' is out of scope for the sandbox — main agent should tell user to do it locally with their own git credentials.
critical_code_review_comments: Dependency fix (date-fns ^3.6.0 + react-day-picker ^9.4.0) is safe at runtime — the shadcn calendar.jsx that uses react-day-picker is never imported anywhere in the actual app, so the v8→v9 API drift (DayPicker props renamed) doesn't crash anything. If the calendar component is ever imported later, its Chevron/IconLeft/IconRight props and classNames will need to be updated for v9 API.
RegisterPage.jsx had two testid mismatches with the spec: 'reg-email-input' → renamed to 'register-email-input', 'role-driver-btn' → renamed to 'register-role-driver'. Fixed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: date-fns / react-day-picker dependency fix verified — no runtime crashes. All 7 register testids now present. All 7 admin tabs present. Driver toggle works (go-live-btn shows when OFFLINE, go-offline-btn when LIVE). Passenger leaflet map renders. Only cosmetic warnings remaining: (a) DriverPage useEffect missing dep, (b) 401 spam from /api/auth/me on public pages, (c) hydration <span>-in-<option> warning. None are regressions from this dep bump.
rca of the issue: N/A — this iteration was a dependency-fix regression check, not a bug fix. Original Vercel ERESOLVE was caused by date-fns@4 (unsupported by react-day-picker@8). Chosen fix (date-fns@^3.6.0 + react-day-picker@^9.4.0) is compatible with React 19 as well. Verified no runtime import of react-day-picker occurs from the running app (calendar.jsx is dead code), so v9 API changes don't affect current UX.
